properties.name: Ōmaha Bay Temporary Closure
properties.species_restricted: Seaweed and all invertebrates except kina, spiny rock lobster, and scallop
properties.species_allowed: Finfish, kina, spiny rock lobster, scallop
properties.customary_fishing: Customary fishing under Regulations 50-52 and Kaimoana Customary Fishing Regulations is not restricted
properties.source: NZ Gazette: Fisheries (Ōmaha Bay, Kawau Bay, and Whangaparāoa Peninsula Temporary Closure) Notice 2026
properties.notes: 200m offshore from mean high-water mark. Includes waters within 200m of Panetiki Island/The Outpost and Te Kohuroa/Mathesons Bay island. Excludes Whangateau Harbour. Extends from Cape Rodney south past Ti Point to the western boundary of Tāwharanui Marine Reserve.

properties.name: Kawau Bay Temporary Closure
properties.species_restricted: Seaweed and all invertebrates except kina, spiny rock lobster, and scallop
properties.species_allowed: Finfish, kina, spiny rock lobster, scallop
properties.customary_fishing: Customary fishing under Regulations 50-52 and Kaimoana Customary Fishing Regulations is not restricted
properties.source: NZ Gazette: Fisheries (Ōmaha Bay, Kawau Bay, and Whangaparāoa Peninsula Temporary Closure) Notice 2026
properties.notes: 200m offshore from mean high-water mark. Two sub-areas separated by Matakana River mouth.

properties.name: Whangaparāoa Peninsula Temporary Closure
properties.species_restricted: Seaweed and all invertebrates except kina, spiny rock lobster, and scallop
properties.species_allowed: Finfish, kina, spiny rock lobster, scallop
properties.customary_fishing: Customary fishing under Regulations 50-52 and Kaimoana Customary Fishing Regulations is not restricted
properties.source: NZ Gazette: Fisheries (Ōmaha Bay, Kawau Bay, and Whangaparāoa Peninsula Temporary Closure) Notice 2026
properties.notes: 200m offshore from mean high-water mark along north coast. Excludes Long Bay-Okura Marine Reserve.

properties.name: Waiheke Island Temporary Closure
properties.species_restricted: Mussels (green-lipped, black, horse), rock lobster (red and packhorse), pāua (blackfoot and yellowfoot)
properties.source: NZ Gazette: Fisheries (Waiheke Island Temporary Closure) Notice 2024
properties.notes: 1 nautical mile from mean high-water mark of Waiheke Island. Includes Pakatoa Island. Excludes Te Matuku Marine Reserve.

properties.name: Umupuia Beach Temporary Closure
properties.source: NZ Gazette: Fisheries (Umupuia Beach Temporary Closure) Notice 2024
properties.notes: Coastal closure between Te Puea and Whakakaiwhara Point. Cockles only.

properties.name: Te Mātā and Waipatukahu Temporary Closure
properties.species_restricted: Pipi, cockles, mussels (green-lipped, black, freshwater, horse), oysters (Pacific, rock, dredge)
properties.source: NZ Gazette: Fisheries (Te Mātā and Waipatukahu Temporary Closure) Notice 2024
properties.notes: 1 nautical mile offshore from mean high-water mark between Ōtaki Creek mouth and Diehard Stream mouth.

properties.name: Rakaumangamanga Rāhui Tapu (Maunganui Bay – Oke Bay)
properties.species_restricted: All fish, aquatic life, and seaweed except kina
properties.species_allowed: Kina (Evechinus chloroticus). Non-commercial Māori customary fishing with permit
properties.source: Northland Regional Council — Te Hā o Tangaroa Protection Areas (Regional Plan)
properties.notes: Rāhui tapu no-take area from Maunganui Bay to Opourua (Oke Bay). Previously a s186A temporary closure since 2010, now established under the Regional Plan. Penalties up to $300,000 or 2 years imprisonment.

properties.name: Mimiwhangata Rāhui Tapu
properties.species_restricted: All fish, aquatic life, and seaweed except kina
properties.species_allowed: Kina (Evechinus chloroticus). Non-commercial Māori customary fishing with permit
properties.source: Northland Regional Council — Te Hā o Tangaroa Protection Areas (Regional Plan)
properties.notes: Rāhui tapu no-take area at Mimiwhangata peninsula. Southern boundary extends 6.2km offshore from Tauranga Kawau Point. Penalties up to $300,000 or 2 years imprisonment.

properties.name: Ngā Au o Morunga Mai Rakaumangamanga Protection Area
properties.species_restricted: Commercial bottom trawling, bottom pair trawling, Danish seining, and purse seining prohibited to 100m depth
properties.species_allowed: All recreational fishing. Commercial fishing by other methods allowed
properties.source: Northland Regional Council — Te Hā o Tangaroa Protection Areas (Regional Plan)
properties.notes: Commercial fishing restriction area around Cape Brett. Does not affect recreational fishing, kina harvest limits, or Māori customary fishing rights.

properties.name: Te Puna Mātaitai Reserve
properties.species_restricted: No commercial fishing. Mussel harvest closure (bylaw).
properties.notes: Mātaitai reserve — Tangata Kaitiaki manage customary and recreational take. ~20 sq km.
properties.customary_fishing: Tangata Kaitiaki/Tiaki appointed under the Fisheries (Kaimoana Customary Fishing) Regulations 1998

properties.name: Te Rāwhiti Exotic Caulerpa High Risk Zone
properties.species_restricted: No fishing, anchoring, or removal of marine life (caulerpa biosecurity)
properties.gazette_notice: Controlled Area Notice under Biosecurity Act 1993
properties.notes: CAN Red Zone — high risk exotic caulerpa area. No anchoring, no fishing, no removal of organisms.

properties.name: Ngāti Manuhiri Rāhui — Hauturu-o-Toi / Little Barrier Island
properties.species_restricted: No anchoring without permit. No seabed-contact fishing (netting, dredging, trawling, potting)
properties.gazette_notice: Cultural rāhui — CAN is the legal instrument
properties.notes: Rāhui placed by Ngāti Manuhiri alongside MPI Controlled Area Notice. Entire marine perimeter. Updated to 1.8 km offshore buffer from coastline-following island perimeter.
properties.update_note: Outer boundary rebuilt as 1.8 km offshore buffer from inner land perimeter.

properties.name: Ngāti Rehua Ngātiwai ki Aotea Rāhui Tapū
properties.species_restricted: All marine life (associated with caulerpa response)
properties.notes: Rāhui tapū over 4 west coast harbours: Tryphena (Rangitāwhiri), Blind Bay/Okupu (Ō-Kupe-Mai-Tawhiti), Whangaparapara, Port Fitzroy (Te Whanga-o-Rarohara).
